definition: Central bank independence is eroding through four demographically conditioned channels: sympathetic governor appointments (Kuroda template), mandate expansion, $1T+ interest payments making rate decisions de facto fiscal policy (novel structural observation), and aging electorates tolerating inflation more than recession. Market discipline partially constrains (UK gilt crisis).
evidence: $1T+ interest payments create mechanical inseparability of monetary and fiscal policy
Aging electorates: net transfer recipients tolerate inflation over recession
UK gilt crisis shows market discipline partially constrains
open_questions: How does the $1T+ interest payment channel evolve as debt levels change?
Can institutional design prevent erosion despite demographic pressure?
Does the channel vary across different central bank mandates and structures?
